revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24608848913|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:青塚古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Aozuka Kofun |image=[[File:Aozuka Kofun, Kannonji.jpg|300px]] |location={{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, [[:en:Kan'onji|Kan'onji]] City, Hara Town 27-28 |coordinates=34|coordinates=7|coordinates=1.2 |coordinates=133|coordinates=42|coordinates=20.8 |type=[[Scallop-shaped Kofun|Scallop-shaped tumulus]] |area=Total length 60 m |built={{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} mid |burial=[[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] |Buried individual= |burial goods found= |alternate_name=City-Historic Site1 (March 12, 1970) |notes= }} '''Aozuka Kofun''' (青塚古墳) is a mid-{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|qid=Q861270|lt=Kan'onji, Kagawa|1=Kan'onji, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. == Overview == [[File:青塚古墳 (観音寺市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|{{center|excavations|qq=}}{{small|On display at the Kan'onji City Furusato Museum.|qq=}}]] It is located at 27 and 28 Hara-chō in Kan'onji City. Dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, it is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with an estimated main-axis length of about 60 meters. However, the front part has disappeared and only the rear circular portion remains, measuring about 43 meters in diameter. Traces of a surrounding moat have also been identified. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-type stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-type stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, which contains a carved-out {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}}. The coffin stone material has been identified as welded tuff from {{ill|Mount Aso|en|Mount Aso|qid=Q733710|lt=Aso|1=Mount Aso}}. A similar boat-shaped Sarcophagus—also made of welded tuff from Mount Aso—has been discovered at the Maruyama Kofun, a circular tumulus in Dōmoto-chō within the same city. Additionally, a survey conducted by {{ill|Tokushima Bunri University|en|Tokushima Bunri University|qid=Q581252|lt=Tokushima Bunri University|1=Tokushima Bunri University}} of the {{ill|Mikuriya Tenjinsha Kofun|ja|御厨天神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuriya Tenjinsha Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|qid=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu, Kagawa|1=Takamatsu}}, dating to the late 5th century, confirmed that it too has a scallop-shaped layout. == Municipal other_Historic Site == On March 12, 1970 (Shōwa 45), it was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Kan'onji City; following the 2005 (Heisei 17) municipal merger, it remains a municipal historic site under the new city administration. The managing organization is {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|qid=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}}, and even today a {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=Torii|1=Torii}} stands in front of the tumulus, with the entire kofun regarded as sacred ground. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} * {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/info/k12/070208-2.html List of Designated other_Historic Site of Kan'onji City] (Kan'onji City Hall) * [http://kgw.bunri-u.ac.jp/bunzai/active/mimaya/ Survey of Takamatsu City Mikuriya Tenjinsha Kofun] (Tokushima Bunri University, Faculty of Letters, Department of Cultural Property Studies) {{Japanese-history-stub|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
